title: Iran malaria control and eradication campaign, 1945-1990 — narrative timeline with embedded statistics (institutional milestones, peak case-burden estimate, geographic burden concentration)
source_org: Compiled from WHO EMRO malaria strategy pages, WHO results-report country story, endmalaria.org/Global Fund 'Iran Story' summary, and general academic secondary literature, via WebSearch synthesis (primary underlying documents were blocked, see failures below)
frequency: irregular narrative milestones, NOT a continuous annual case-count series
units: described per-row in CSV; narrative txt for qualitative context
license_terms: Factual citation of WHO and academic-summary public content; no verbatim reproduction beyond short attributed figures
notes: Directly addresses the user's explicit candidate direction #2 example ('major disease incidence, e.g. malaria eradication campaign statistics'). Follows the narrative-embedded-stats precedent set at data/raw/pahlavi-era-primary-extraction/iranica-fiscal-system-narrative-series-1921-1979/ (a .txt narrative file with citations, plus a companion .csv of the numeric anchors extractable from that narrative) BECAUSE the primary quantitative sources for this topic (CDC's archived 'Malaria control in Iran' document and a ResearchGate academic paper with the fullest case-count time series) were both blocked (HTTP 403) in this pass -- see failures. This is honestly a THINNER dataset than the other five produced in this hunt: it has real, cross-confirmed institutional milestones (1945 training course, 1947 first DDT pilot, 1957 national programme launch, 1980 eradication-to-control policy shift, 1990 three-province residual burden) and one genuinely striking order-of-magnitude estimate (5 million cases/year at peak burden, imprecisely dated to roughly the late 1950s) but does NOT have a continuous annual case-count series, which is what would be needed to chart malaria incidence over time the way, e.g., the FAOSTAT agricultural-production series can be charted. Flagged explicitly in both files rather than papering over the gap with fabricated interpolated values. WHO's modern-era malaria elimination data (2000s-present) is NOT re-covered here since the existing data/raw/who-health-econ/ and data/processed/owid_indicators.csv sources likely already have modern disease-burden indicators covering this period at the indicator-database level -- this dataset's unique value is specifically the pre-1990, Pahlavi-era-through-early-IRI institutional history that those modern indicator databases don't reach back to.

failures.result: certificate has expired -- could not retrieve, would have been a useful cross-check for broader pre/post-1979 social statistics beyond just malaria

failures.url: https://iran.un.org/en/23440-history-successful-struggle-against-malaria-islamic-republic-iran (the actual full PDF report, 'History of the successful struggle against malaria in the Islamic Republic of Iran', published with Iran's Ministry of Health and Medical Education + Global Fund)
failures.result: landing page returned only the publication abstract/download-count metadata, not the underlying PDF content or its statistical tables -- this is very likely the single best available primary source for a full annual case-count series and is flagged as the top candidate for a future continuation pass
